codeReview.summary: The implementation cleanly extends BackendConfig with an OAuthConfig struct (client_id, scopes, token_path, auth_url, token_url), relaxes config validation for OAuth backends (copilot/codex don't require api_key), adds a type switch in Registry.LoadFromConfig() for openai/copilot/codex/unknown, adds Get/Has/Names helper methods, and supports hot-reload by replacing the entire backend map on config change. Test coverage is thorough: 22 config tests and 15 registry tests covering all expected behaviors including hot-reload add/remove/update, mixed backend types, YAML parsing, validation edge cases, and multiple independent OAuth backends. The code follows project conventions (error wrapping, naming, imports). No blocking issues found.

issues.description: Validate() defaults Type to 'openai' and then checks IsOAuthBackend() on c.Backends[i] — this works correctly but relies on mutating the input slice. If Validate() is called multiple times on the same Config, the second call will see the mutated Type='openai' from the first call. This is benign in practice (Parse creates a fresh Config each time, and Validate is called from Parse), but could be surprising if someone calls Validate() directly on a long-lived Config object. Not blocking because the current usage pattern is safe.

IsOAuthBackend() uses a hardcoded switch statement for 'copilot' and 'codex'. If a new OAuth backend type is added in the future, this method must be updated. A more extensible approach would be to check if OAuth config is present (bc.OAuth != nil) instead. However, the current approach is explicit and clear, which is arguably better for maintainability at this stage. Not blocking.

LoadFromConfig() replaces the entire backends map on every call (r.backends = make(map[string]Backend)). This means in-flight requests to backends that are about to be removed could continue using their old backend reference. This is acceptable behavior since the Backend interface doesn't have a Close/Shutdown method, and the old backends will be GC'd once no references remain. The alternative (diff-based add/remove) would be more complex without clear benefit. Not blocking.

Validate() counts enabled backends first in a separate pass, then iterates again. These could be merged into a single pass to avoid iterating the backends slice twice. This is a minor efficiency concern and has no correctness impact.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker used heredoc (cat > file << 'EOF') to write entire source files instead of using the Create or Edit tools. This pattern works but makes it harder to review incremental changes. The AGENTS.md doesn't explicitly state to prefer incremental edits over full file rewrites.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows multiple 'cat > file << 'EOF'' commands for writing config.go and registry.go. Handoff shows the worker rewrote entire files rather than using surgical edits.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker deviated from the go-backend-worker skill by writing tests alongside implementation instead of TDD (tests first). The deviation was justified — tests and implementation were tightly coupled. The skill might benefit from more flexible guidance on TDD for structurally intertwined changes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.followedProcedure = false, deviation on step '3. Write Tests First (Red)', worker wrote tests in parallel with implementation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered and documented that the copilot and codex backends initially use OpenAI-compatible placeholders until actual implementations are built. This pattern was correctly recorded in the library file oauth-config-and-registry.md, which is good.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Library file at .factory/library/oauth-config-and-registry.md documents the placeholder pattern and notes that copilot/codex currently use OpenAI backend as placeholder.

summary: The oauth-config-and-registry feature is well-implemented with clean code, thorough test coverage (37 tests across config and backend packages), and proper adherence to project conventions. The OAuthConfig struct has all required fields, validation correctly relaxes the api_key requirement for copilot/codex backends while keeping base_url mandatory, the Registry.LoadFromConfig() type switch handles all backend types with proper error handling for unknowns, and hot-reload works by replacing the entire backend map. No blocking issues found. The only notable observations are minor: Validate() mutates the input slice when defaulting Type to 'openai' (benign in current usage), and IsOAuthBackend() uses a hardcoded type list rather than checking for OAuth config presence (explicit but less extensible). All tests pass in the current codebase.
